meetings.summary: Politically loaded meeting. 4 notices of motion (constitutional maximum), 5 member questions, 2 public questions. Key battlegrounds: potholes motion directly targets Reform election promise; care homes questions test day centre commitment; highways budget question from Conservative leader.

standing_orders_summary.friendly_amendment_rule: Motion mover may amend their own motion or accept an amendment without debate or vote
standing_orders_summary.motions_that_fall_rule: Any motion not disposed of at Full Council falls. Does not carry forward to next meeting.

motions.title: Fylde Borough: Tarnbrick Farm Green Waste Composting
motions.full_text: The Development Control Committee is asked that planning permission be granted subject to the conditions set out in Appendix 'A'.
motions.opposition_strategy: Opposition will likely focus on the retrospective nature of part of the application, potential negative impacts on rural character, and amenity concerns for nearby residents (noise, dust, traffic, visual impact). They may question the effectiveness of proposed conditions to mitigate these impacts or advocate for refusal/deferral.
motions.ruling_group_response: Highlight that the site is well-established, the proposal aims to improve efficiency rather than intensify operations. Emphasise the robust conditions in Appendix 'A' designed to mitigate impacts, such as secure sheeting for vehicles, mud/dust control, stockpile height limits, specific operating hours, white noise reversing alarms, and a Habitat Management and Monitoring Plan. Stress the economic benefits and compliance with planning policy.
key_data_points: Change of use of 1.8 hectares of agricultural land to green waste composting, partly retrospective
New portal-frame building (60.9m x 24.3m x 10m) for processing/storage and workshop
Extension to existing storage building (18.2m x 12.4m x 9.6m)
New concrete internal access road (150m x 4m)
Nearest residential properties (Parkinson Lane) approximately 150 metres away

Conditions include: secure sheeting, mud/dust control, 3m stockpile height limit, matching building materials, restricted operating hours (Mon-Fri 0800-1800, Sat/Sun/PH 0800-1200), white noise reversing alarms, dust control, Habitat Management and Monitoring Plan (30 years), landscaping scheme.

motions.title: Hyndburn Borough: 9 Harwood Lane Children's Care Home
motions.full_text: That planning permission be granted subject to the conditions set out in appendix A relating to the following matters: time limits; approved plans and documents; use limited to a children's care home as proposed and no other use within Use Class C2.
motions.opposition_strategy: Opposition may raise 'Not In My Backyard' (NIMBY) concerns, citing the three letters of objection received. They will likely focus on potential impacts on residential amenity, such as increased traffic, noise, and the perceived change in character of the area. They might argue that the location is inappropriate for a care home.
motions.ruling_group_response: Emphasise the critical need for accommodation for looked after children, aligning with national planning policy and the 'Planning for accommodation for looked after children' statement. Highlight the property's suitability as a detached house with a private garden and ample off-street parking (6 vehicles). Stress the robust conditions limiting the use to only two children and prohibiting any other Use Class C2 purpose, ensuring minimal impact on the local area. Frame the decision as supporting 'Better lives for all' and 'stronger communities'.
key_data_points: Change of use from C3 dwelling house to C2 residential institution
Home for up to two young persons (11-17 years old)
No more than two carers staying overnight, up to four staff during the day on a rota system
Minor internal layout change to create an additional office space
Detached house with three to four bedrooms, private enclosed garden, and off-street parking for 6 vehicles

motions.title: Delegated Development Control Decisions
motions.full_text: The Development Control Committee is asked to note to the report.
motions.opposition_strategy: This is a noting item, so direct opposition is unlikely. However, opposition members may use this as an opportunity to ask questions about specific delegated decisions, particularly the retrospective planning application for Clayton Hall Sand Quarry, or to generally question the scope and transparency of delegated powers.
motions.ruling_group_response: Reiterate that the report is for noting only and that all decisions were taken in accordance with the County Council's Scheme of Delegation. Offer to provide detailed information on any specific application offline if members have further queries. Keep responses concise and factual.
key_data_points: Prior approval for demolition of caretaker's house at Ryelands Community Primary School, Lancaster
EIA Screening Opinion for Wigan and Skelmersdale Waste Water Treatment Works upgrade
EIA Screening Opinion for retrospective amendment of conditions at Clayton Hall Sand Quarry, Chorley
Installation of additional boundary security fence at Ightenhill Primary School, Burnley.

questions.topic: General planning matters or specific agenda items
questions.question_text: No specific public questions provided in documents. Anticipate questions related to the Tarnbrick Farm composting site (noise, traffic, environmental impact) or the Harwood Lane children's home (local amenity, traffic, suitability of location).
recommended_answer_points: For Tarnbrick Farm: Refer to the robust conditions in place to mitigate impacts. Emphasise the existing nature of the site and the efficiency improvements.
For Harwood Lane: Highlight the need for children's homes, national policy support, and specific conditions limiting use and impact. Stress the property's suitability and parking provisions.
For general questions: Provide factual, concise answers. Offer to follow up with detailed information if required.
questions.trap_explanation: Public questions can be used to amplify local concerns or to introduce contentious issues not directly on the agenda. Be prepared for emotional appeals or attempts to draw committee members into detailed debate outside of the formal motion structure.

tactical_scenarios.scenario: Opposition member attempts to defer a planning application decision (e.g., Tarnbrick Farm or Harwood Lane) for further information or site visit.
tactical_scenarios.counter: The Chair should remind the committee that all necessary information is before them and that officers have provided comprehensive reports. Stress the importance of making a decision based on the presented evidence. If a deferral motion is formally moved, ensure the ruling group votes against it, unless there is a clear, evidenced deficiency in the report that genuinely warrants deferral.
tactical_scenarios.standing_order: Motion debate rules apply. Mover 5 min, Others 3 min.

tactical_scenarios.scenario: Call for a recorded vote on a contentious planning application (e.g., Tarnbrick Farm or Harwood Lane).
tactical_scenarios.counter: Be prepared for a recorded vote. Ensure all ruling group members are present and understand the rationale for supporting the officer recommendation. A recorded vote will consume additional time but is a legitimate procedural tool. The Chair should manage the process efficiently.
tactical_scenarios.standing_order: N/A (specific SO for recorded votes not provided, but it's a standard council procedure).

tactical_scenarios.scenario: Opposition members use their 3-minute speaking slots to make lengthy, repetitive, or irrelevant points, attempting to delay proceedings or exhaust debate time.
tactical_scenarios.counter: The Chair must strictly enforce the 3-minute time limit for speakers. Remind members to stick to the specific item under debate. If a member is repetitive, the Chair can intervene and ask them to conclude their remarks. Do not allow 'points of order' to be used as a means to extend debate.

time_management.recommended_time_saving: The Chair should strictly enforce speaking time limits (3 min for members) for each agenda item. Officer presentations should be concise and focus on key points. Avoid allowing repetitive questions or comments.
